data.title: Trustline Advisory Group introduces AI Reputation & Visibility Service to aid companies in enhancing AI search presence.
data.description: A new advisory service focused on AI Reputation and Visibility has been introduced by Trustline Advisory Group in London on Feb. 12, 2026. This initiative is designed to assist companies in enhancing their representation on AI-driven search platforms and prominent language models. The advisory aims to improve companies' visibility and reputation in the digital realm, catering to the evolving strategies required in the technological landscape. This introduction signifies a shift towards addressing the significance of AI in online presence management.

data.title: UT Health San Antonio receives $5 million donation from Bill and Rebecca Reed for Biggs Institute targeting dementia.
data.description: UT Health San Antonio has received a generous donation of $5 million from Bill and Rebecca Reed to enhance the Glenn Biggs Institute for Alzheimer's. The funds will be used to further research, therapies, and care for dementia. This contribution signifies a significant step towards combatting Alzheimer's and related diseases.

data.title: Daniel Stark Injury Lawyers and La Vega ISD collaborate to enhance child passenger safety in Waco, Texas, on Feb 12, 2026.
data.description: A collaboration has been formed between Daniel Stark Injury Lawyers and La Vega Independent School District in Waco, Texas. The partnership, established on February 12, 2026, aims to enhance child passenger safety in the region. This initiative intends to bolster existing efforts in Waco with regard to ensuring the safety of young passengers. The joint endeavor signifies a collective commitment to prioritizing safety and promoting best practices.

data.title: Guardant Health offers grants for up to 100 Shield Colorectal Cancer Blood Tests to support screening events, applying groups invite.
data.description: To raise awareness for Colorectal Cancer (CRC) in March, patient and public health organizations are encouraged to apply for up to 100 Shield Colorectal Cancer blood tests in Palo Alto, California. The tests will help in community screening events and educational outreach initiatives. The deadline for applications is approaching, and successful groups will be provided with the tests to support their efforts during CRC Awareness Month. The initiative aims to increase awareness and early detection of CRC through partnerships with organizations dedicated to public health.

data.title: Neurocrine Biosciences reports Q4 earnings of $1.88/share, $805.5M sales, Ingrezza sales hit $657.5M; Crenessity sales at $135.3M.
data.description: Neurocrine Biosciences Inc recently released its fourth-quarter financial results. The adjusted earnings were $1.88 per share, slightly lower than the expected $1.89 per share. Sales for the quarter amounted to $805.5 million, surpassing the consensus of $792.47 million. Net product sales for the fourth quarter reached $798.3 million, marking a 29% increase from the previous year. Specifically, their drug Ingrezza, used for involuntary movements, saw net product sales of $657.5 million for the quarter and $2.51 billion for the full year of 2025, indicating growth of 7% and 9% year-over-year, respectively. The company attributed this success to a rise in prescription volume, driven by strong patient demand. On the other hand, Crenessity, a classic congenital adrenal hyperplasia drug, achieved net product sales of $135.3 million for the quarter and $301.2 million for the full year 2025. This was fueled by the enrollment of 431 and 2,048 new patients, showcasing robust demand. The results were impacted by strategic investments in formulary access, which led to a lower net price but are expected to facilitate long-term growth.

data.title: Growing institutional interest and network activity indicate Ether may rise to $2.4K despite weak price action.
data.description: Despite Ethereum experiencing a decline in its price movement, an increase in institutional investor participation and a significant rise in network activity indicate that Ether is establishing a foundation for a potential surge towards $2,400.

data.title: Heinemann's Saxon Reading Foundations approved by Oklahoma State Dept of Education for meeting literacy standards.
data.description: Heinemann revealed today in Portsmouth, New Hampshire, that their Saxon Reading Foundations program has received approval from the Oklahoma State Department of Education (OSDE) for meeting the state's standards for top-notch literacy intervention resources. The recognition signifies that Saxon Reading Foundations has satisfied the mandatory criteria set forth by OSDE in Oklahoma for such materials.

data.title: West Coast Community Bancorp adds 2 new directors, retiring one after a long service. Feb. 12, 2026.
data.description: In Santa Cruz, California on February 12, 2026, West Coast Community Bancorp, identified as Bancorp and trading under the ticker symbol OTCQX: WCCB, announced the appointment of Gunlek L. Ruder and Alexander B. Potts to serve on the Boards of Directors for both the Bank and Bancorp. West Coast Community Bank operates under this parent company.

data.title: Justin Carter joins Sync(d) AI as Founder and Partner to drive organizations to embrace AI and automation.
data.description: Justin Carter has been appointed as Founder and Partner at Sync(d) AI, a Frontier AI company based in Denver. The organization aims to revolutionize how businesses operate on Microsoft Business Applications by integrating AI, automation, and promoting business agility. This move is significant in the industry as it showcases a commitment towards implementing cutting-edge technologies to improve efficiency and productivity. The announcement was made on February 12, 2026, marking a new chapter for Sync(d) AI under Justin's leadership. This development highlights a strategic shift towards embracing innovative solutions in the corporate world.

data.title: Dr. Dana Anderson of Infleqtion elected to National Academy of Engineering for quantum tech contributions. Company plans to go public through merger with Churchill Capital Corp X.
data.description: In Boulder, Colorado, Infleqtion, a prominent company in quantum sensing and computing, is known for its advancements in atom-based quantum technology catered towards commercial and governmental uses. Dr. Dana Anderson has been recognized for his contributions to optical quantum engineering of ultracold atoms with an induction into the esteemed National Academy of Engineering (NAE). Infleqtion has revealed intentions of becoming public through a merger with Churchill Capital Corp X (NASDAQ: CCCX). This development marks a significant milestone for the company.

data.title: Physicians and leaders gather in Texas for IMA's 5th Annual Medical Education Conference in April. Early bird pricing until Feb 28.
data.description: A two-day continuing medical education (CME) event is set to focus on innovation, ethics, and emerging trends in medicine. The Independent Medical Alliance (IMA) revealed details about its 5th Annual Medical Education Conference, scheduled for February 12, 2026, in Washington. This accredited event will spotlight key developments in the field and offer insights into future trends. To avail of Early Bird pricing, interested participants must register before February 28.

data.title: PAR Technology Corporation (NYSE: PAR) to report Q4 2025 financial results on Feb 26, 2026, 4:00 p.m. ET with investor call at 4:30 p.m. ET. Webcast available.
data.description: PAR Technology Corporation (NYSE: PAR) from New Hartford, N.Y. revealed that it is set to disclose its fourth-quarter financial results on February 26, 2026, at 4:00 p.m. ET. Following this, an investor presentation and conference call will take place at 4:30 p.m. ET. The live webcast of the earnings conference call can be accessed through the PAR Technology Investor Relations website at http://www.partech.com/investor-relations/.

data.title: Correction: Sony Electronics launches WF-1000XM6 truly wireless earbuds for premium sound quality and noise-canceling.
data.description: Sony Electronics has recently unveiled the WF-1000XM6 Truly Wireless Earbuds, boasting top-tier noise-canceling technology and premium sound quality. The announcement was made on February 12, 2026. The new earbuds are setting new standards in the market with their exceptional noise-canceling capabilities. These earbuds are equipped with state-of-the-art features to ensure unparalleled sound performance, offering users an immersive listening experience.
